rationale: A precision strike will exploit our military readiness and the enemy’s distraction, degrading their command capabilities without triggering full escalation.
Forward-deploying our fleet off the southern coast pressures Tethys economically and signals resolve amid their shipping inspections.
A rapid, shallow incursion seizes key terrain and shatters their defensive momentum, demonstrating our dominance on the ground.
A coordinated cyber strike on Tethys’ port logistics systems will amplify the disruption from their own shipping inspections and degrade their economic resilience amid rising oil prices.
Disabling key oil export infrastructure under Tethys control will capitalize on their distraction and reduce their revenue windfall from the price surge, weakening their war effort.
Orchestrating a false flag incident that exposes alleged Tethys collusion with Novaris will undermine their international support and counter their recent information warfare gains.
Targeting key Tethys energy and shipping elites will amplify economic pressure from rising oil prices and complement Krasnov’s naval deployment by disrupting their revenue streams.
Seizing offshore assets of Tethys-linked oligarchs undermines their war financing and reinforces Morozov’s sabotage efforts by reducing funds available for infrastructure repair.
Mild devaluation of our currency relative to Tethys’ bolsters our export competitiveness and absorbs some sanction impacts, stabilizing domestic costs amid Petrova’s recommended caution on inflation.
A highly visible mobilization of armored units along the forward line will signal resolve and exploit Novaris’s slowing offensive momentum.
Deploying our corvette fleet near contested waters underscores sovereignty and counters the major power’s intrusive shipping inspections.
Persistent drone surveillance behind enemy lines is critical to identifying supply chokepoints for future precision strikes.
Given the high crisis level and ongoing enemy cyber operations, securing our critical military and energy infrastructure is essential to maintain operational continuity.
Proactive counterespionage measures will disrupt Novaris’s infiltration attempts and protect the integrity of our defense and political leadership.
Sharing verified threat data with Tethys strengthens alliance cohesion and enables coordinated defense against joint cyber and hybrid threats.

Given the moderate sanctions level and strong international support, we can credibly impose targeted sanctions on key Novaris energy and defense elites to amplify economic pressure without triggering overreach.
Seizing assets of sanctioned Novaris entities in our jurisdiction will disrupt their financial networks and signal resolve, complementing military and intelligence-led deterrence efforts.
Locking in preferential trade terms with Tethys now will stabilize our supply chains and reward alignment, reinforcing our economic resilience amid ongoing naval and energy disruptions.
Establishing neutral humanitarian corridors demonstrates moral leadership, reinforces our legitimacy amid high crisis conditions, and aligns with international expectations given our 65% support.
Deepening institutional ties with Tethys through formal partnership frameworks amplifies collective pressure on Novaris while anchoring us within liberal international structures.

Extending a calibrated offer of mediation—framed as a peace-seeking initiative—positions us as a responsible actor and exploits divisions in Novaris’s command structure revealed by recent intelligence leaks.
